title: Fix off-by-one binary search upper bound

input.prompt:
bisect.py implements bisect_left but fails on tail insert and empty-slot insert because hi is shrunk and a post-loop correction masks some cases. Fix minimally so:
- bisect_left([1,3,5], 6) == 3
- bisect_left([1,3,5], 0) == 0
- bisect_left([], 1) == 0
Keep function name and signature. Print DONE.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Surgical fix to invariant-breaking search bounds.

title: Fix forward iteration with in-loop pop skipping elements

input.prompt: filter_odds.py must return [1,3] for remove_evens([1,2,3,4]) and [1,3,5] for remove_evens([2,1,4,3,6,5]). Current forward pop skips elements after deletion. Fix algorithm only; keep function name remove_evens. Print DONE.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Classic in-loop mutation bug that passes some inputs and fails others.

title: Fix reentrant counter guard that double-releases lock

input.prompt: counter.py Guard should allow nested with Guard(): blocks on one thread, but must release the lock exactly once on outermost exit. Fix without changing public Guard API. Print DONE.

files.counter.py:
import threading

_lock = threading.RLock()
_count = 0


class Guard:
    def __enter__(self):
        _lock.acquire()
        return self

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc, tb):
        _lock.release()
        _lock.release()  # bug: double release on nested exit
        return False


def inc():
    global _count
    with Guard():
        with Guard():
            _count += 1

metadata.mainstream_gap: Reentrancy bookkeeping error invisible to single-level tests.

title: Replace SQL string interpolation with parameter binding

input.prompt: queries.py builds SQL with f-strings (unsafe). Refactor get_user_by_name to use parameterized execute(query, params) while preserving function signature and return shape. Do not change fetch_one. Print DONE.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Security fix requiring API-preserving refactor, not greenfield query writing.

title: Fix Floyd tortoise-hare cycle detection step size

input.prompt: cycle.py has has_cycle on a singly linked list given as arrays (values, next indices, -1 for null). It fails on 1->2->3->1 because the fast pointer advances only one node per loop instead of two. Fix only the fast advance so it moves two nodes per loop. Print DONE.

metadata.mainstream_gap: Subtle two-pointer invariant break that still terminates on acyclic lists.

title: Replace gather when sibling tasks must be cancelled on failure

input.prompt: pipeline.py assumes that if publish_primary() fails, publish_audit() is automatically stopped. That assumption is wrong for asyncio.gather with return_exceptions=False. Refactor publish_all so a failure in one child cancels the sibling before publish_all exits. Prefer structured concurrency. Print DONE.

files.pipeline.py:
import asyncio


async def publish_all(publish_primary, publish_audit):
    await asyncio.gather(
        publish_primary(),
        publish_audit(),
    )

metadata.mainstream_gap: asyncio.gather does not cancel sibling awaitables on first exception; agents often preserve the bug.

title: Remove NOT IN filter that misbehaves when blocked IDs contain NULL

input.prompt: filters.py generates a PostgreSQL exclusion predicate. blocked_ids may contain NULL, so `user_id NOT IN (...)` can filter out everything by becoming unknown. Rewrite build_exclusion_predicate to use a NULL-safe NOT EXISTS pattern over the provided placeholder table alias `blocked(id)`. Keep the function name and argument name. Print DONE.

files.filters.py:
def build_exclusion_predicate(column: str) -> str:
    return f"{column} NOT IN (SELECT id FROM blocked)"

metadata.mainstream_gap: SQL three-valued NULL logic breaks naive exclusion filters.
